subject: History
title: The Rise of Nationalism in Europe

questions.text: Who was the French artist who prepared a series of four prints visualizing his dream of a world made up of 'democratic and social Republics' in 1848?
questions.explanation: Frédéric Sorrieu painted these famous prints to visualize the spread of nation-states and democracy.

questions.text: The Civil Code of 1804 in France is usually known as the:
questions.explanation: Napoleon simplified administrative divisions and established equality before the law through this code.

questions.text: What was the main objective of the Treaty of Vienna of 1815?
options: To restore monarchies overthrown by Napoleon
questions.explanation: The representatives of European powers met at Vienna to undo the changes brought about by the Napoleonic wars and restore conservative orders.

questions.text: The 'Zollverein', formed in 1834 at the initiative of Prussia, was a:
questions.explanation: The Zollverein abolished tariff barriers and reduced the number of currencies, facilitating economic nationalism.

questions.text: Who was described as 'the most dangerous enemy of our social order' by Metternich?
questions.explanation: Metternich viewed Mazzini's relentless opposition to monarchy and vision of democratic republics as a threat to the old order.

questions.text: The term 'Plebiscite' means:
options: A direct vote by which all the people of a region accept or reject a proposal
A system of government by the military
A tax levied on the peasantry
The right to vote given only to property-owning men
questions.explanation: Plebiscite was used to determine the will of the people in various European territories during the 19th century.

questions.text: Which revolution was the first clear expression of nationalism in Europe?
questions.explanation: The French Revolution of 1789 introduced the ideas of 'la patrie' (the fatherland) and 'le citoyen' (the citizen).

questions.text: In 1815, representatives of which European powers met at Vienna to draw up a settlement for Europe?
questions.explanation: These powers had collectively defeated Napoleon and wanted to restore the old order.

questions.text: Who was the Austrian Chancellor who hosted the Congress of Vienna?
questions.explanation: Metternich was a conservative who believed that the monarchy was the only stable form of government.

questions.text: Giuseppe Mazzini founded the secret underground society 'Young Italy' in:
questions.explanation: Mazzini was a key figure in Italian unification and believed in the creation of a unified republic.

questions.text: Which treaty recognized Greece as an independent nation in 1832?
questions.explanation: The Greek War of Independence mobilized nationalist feelings among the educated elite across Europe.

questions.text: The cultural movement which sought to develop a particular form of nationalist sentiment through art and poetry was:
questions.explanation: Romantic artists and poets emphasized emotions, intuition, and mystical feelings over reason.

questions.text: In 1848, an all-German National Assembly met at which city to draft a constitution?
questions.explanation: The assembly met in the Church of St Paul but failed due to lack of support from the monarchy and military.

questions.text: Who was the chief architect of the unification of Germany?
questions.explanation: Bismarck carried out the process with the help of the Prussian army and bureaucracy.

questions.text: Who was proclaimed the German Emperor in 1871 at Versailles?
questions.explanation: The ceremony took place in the Hall of Mirrors after the victory over France.

questions.text: Count Camillo de Cavour was the Prime Minister of which state?
questions.explanation: Cavour was the leading figure in the diplomatic efforts to unify Italy.

questions.text: Who led the 'Expedition of the Thousand' to South Italy?
questions.explanation: Garibaldi and his 'Red Shirts' volunteers successfully liberated the Kingdom of the Two Sicilies.

questions.text: The Act of Union 1707 resulted in the formation of:
options: The United Kingdom of Great Britain
questions.explanation: The act united England and Scotland under one parliament.

questions.text: What was the female allegory representing France called?
questions.explanation: Marianne's characteristics were drawn from those of Liberty and the Republic.

questions.text: In the allegory of Germany, the crown of oak leaves signifies:
questions.explanation: Germania wears a crown of oak leaves, as the German oak stands for heroism.

questions.text: The most serious source of nationalist tension in Europe after 1871 was the region called:
questions.explanation: The Balkans was a region of geographical and ethnic variation under the Ottoman Empire.

questions.text: What is the meaning of 'Utopian'?
options: A vision of society so ideal that it is unlikely to exist
questions.explanation: Sorrieu's prints were described as a utopian vision of the world.

questions.text: Which country was known as the 'cradle of European civilisation'?
questions.explanation: Nationalists in Greece appealed to this sentiment during their struggle against the Ottoman Empire.

questions.text: The term 'Absolutist' refers to:
options: A government with no restraints on power exercised
questions.explanation: In history, it refers to monarchical governments that were highly centralised and repressive.

questions.text: The 'broken chains' symbol represents:
questions.explanation: In nationalist imagery, chains represented slavery or lack of freedom.

questions.text: A 'Nation-state' is one in which:
options: Citizens share a common identity and history
Many different ethnicities live without unity
questions.explanation: The development of nation-states was the goal of 19th-century nationalist movements.

questions.text: The Frankfurt Parliament was convened in the year:
questions.explanation: It was part of the wider revolutionary wave that swept across Europe in 1848.

questions.text: Which language was used by the Polish clergy as a weapon of national resistance against Russian dominance?
questions.explanation: Using Polish in church gatherings and religious instruction was seen as an act of defiance.

questions.text: Who said, 'When France sneezes, the rest of Europe catches cold'?
questions.explanation: Metternich used this to describe the impact of French political events on the continent.

questions.text: Suffrage means:
questions.explanation: Universal suffrage was a major demand of 19th-century liberals.

questions.text: The 'Olive branch around the sword' symbolises:
questions.explanation: It indicated that the nation was strong but preferred peace.

questions.text: Which empire ruled over Austria-Hungary?
questions.explanation: The Habsburg Empire was a patchwork of many different regions and peoples.

questions.text: Liberalism in the economic sphere stood for:
options: Freedom of markets and abolition of state restrictions
questions.explanation: Liberals argued for the removal of barriers to the movement of goods, capital, and people.

questions.text: The Bourbon dynasty, which had been deposed during the French Revolution, was restored to power after 1815.
questions.explanation: The Treaty of Vienna aimed to restore monarchies that had been overthrown by Napoleon.

questions.text: In 1848, the French monarchy was overthrown and replaced by a:
questions.explanation: The revolution led to the proclamation of a Republic based on universal male suffrage.

questions.text: Nationalism aligned with imperialism led Europe to disaster in:
questions.explanation: The competition for colonies and territory among European powers culminated in World War I.

questions.text: The 'Rays of the rising sun' in nationalist imagery symbolises:
questions.explanation: It represents hope and the birth of a new nation.

questions.text: Who was the Prussian King during the German unification?
questions.explanation: He was the head of the Prussian state that led the unification process.

questions.text: The term 'Ethnicity' relates to:
options: Shared racial, cultural or national background
questions.explanation: Ethnic groups share common descent and cultural heritage.

questions.text: Who was proclaimed the first king of unified Italy in 1861?
questions.explanation: Sardinia-Piedmont led the unification process under his leadership.
